title: Windows subprocess UTF-8 decode for operator/verify npm capture
problem: operator_loop --mode execute-safe and verify capture npm/Next.js subprocess output with text=True, which on Windows defaults to cp1252 decoding. UTF-8 output can raise UnicodeDecodeError in background reader threads even when exit code is 0.
acceptance_criteria: Subprocess stdout/stderr capture uses UTF-8 with errors=replace for operator execute-safe and verify.
Non-UTF-8 or mixed binary subprocess output does not crash output capture.
Failing subprocess exit codes still fail verification/operator results.
Output tails remain available in execution/verification reports.
non_goals: Do not change which verification commands are run.
Do not change CI workflow steps.
Do not start cloud provider, embeddings, or concept graph work.
rollback_plan: Revert subprocess_capture helper and restore text=True defaults.
